what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter metadata stub that records an image attachment linked to the design-source channel, pointing at the image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-21 and logging when it was added.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content.
The target is a URI using the image:// scheme: image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-21.
standouts: The target URI uses a custom image:// scheme pointing to design-source/recent-pitches/p-21.
The attachment is scoped to the design-source channel.
The record is purely metadata with a precise ISO timestamp.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter for structured metadata.
Follows an id naming convention att-<hash>.
Uses a channel-memory attachment schema with channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields.
Uses an ISO 8601 timestamp with timezone offset.
References assets via a URI rather than a filesystem path.
concerns: The target URI gives no file extension, MIME type, or size, so the actual image format is not self-evident.
There is no title, caption, alt text, or uploader field to describe the attachment.
There is no checksum or content hash to verify the referenced image.
notes: A minimal, timestamped ledger entry that ties an image asset in the design-source/recent-pitches collection to the channel-memory attachment system.
questions: What image format and dimensions does image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-21 have?
Who added this attachment and why?
What is pitch p-21 and how does this image relate to it?
Does the referenced image asset actually exist at that URI?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file is intended to be an attachment record and it does exactly that—linking an image target to a channel with a timestamp.
apparent_themes: Separation of attachment metadata from asset content
intention_signals: It appears designed to create a durable, auditable pointer from a channel memory entry to a specific image asset, so the attachment can be located and referenced independently of the image file itself.
novelty: The use of a custom image:// URI scheme for local asset references and a dedicated att-* attachment record separate from the referenced media.
contribution: It contributes a dated, channel-scoped reference to an image asset, preserving the attachment's provenance and target location in a form that can be indexed without embedding the image content.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML metadata attachment record with id att-45f9d1db in the design-source channel that points to the image asset image://design-source/recent-pitches/p-21 and records an added timestamp of 2026-06-22T11:31:02.355753+00:00.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to register an image attachment for a recent pitch in the design-source channel so it can be referenced and retrieved through the channel-memory ledger.
